title: Rhaphidospora luzonensis
taxonomy: Rhaphidospora luzonensis belongs to the family Celastraceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the staff vine family. This family consists of around 900 species, found primarily in tropical and subtropical regions. Rhaphidospora luzonensis, specifically, is a species native to the Philippines, classified as a climbing shrub. It is a relatively newly discovered species and its classification is still being refined. More research is needed to understand its specific taxonomic position within the Celastraceae family.
